seasonArc: Romain Grégoire built his 2026 around the late-winter French one-day races and the Ardennes Classics, the terrain that best suits the young Groupama-FDJ puncheur-climber. He opened with a form-building Ruta del Sol, taking 3rd on Stage 5 and 5th overall, before landing his headline win at the Faun Drôme Classic, where he attacked in a two-up move with Matteo Jorgenson and held the Visma rider off in an uphill sprint. Through the cobbled block he rode mostly in support, his profile pointing instead to the hilly Classics, and he arrived at Brabantse Pijl in good shape: he animated the finale, getting into the decisive late lead group on the Overijse circuit before it was caught on the final S-bend, then took 4th in the reduced-group sprint behind Anders Foldager. That aggressive ride set up an Ardennes week in which he featured among the favourites for Amstel, Flèche Wallonne and Liège.
